title: सरस्वती चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ दोहा ॥
जय सरस्वती गुणसदन, विमला वीणा हाथ।
श्वेत कमल पर विराजती, मोरे हों सहनाथ॥
जय सरस्वती भगवती, सकल विद्या दातार।
चालीसा जो पढ़त नित, मिटहिं कष्ट अपार॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय जय सुर मुनि बृन्द बन्दिनी।
मनोहार मतिदायिनी जय जय॥
श्वेत बसन परिधान विराजै।
श्वेत कमल आसन पर साजै॥
वीणा पुस्तक शोभित हाथा।
जपमालिका धरत नित साथा॥
मयूर वाहन शुभ ललामा।
पूजत ब्रह्मा विष्णु शिव कामा॥
राग रागिनी तान सुहावन।
ज्ञान विज्ञान सबै गुण पावन॥
संगीत शास्त्र विनोदिनी मैया।
मूढ़ बुद्धि प्रेरत सदा दैया॥
वेद शास्त्र बोधिनी मैया।
मोह निशा का ज्ञान सवैया॥
जो सरस्वती को ध्यावत।
नित्य प्रेम से गुण गावत॥
मेधा बुद्धि ज्ञान बड़ाई।
विद्या प्रवीण होहिं सुखदाई॥
दासन को विपदा हर लीजै।
ज्ञान बुद्धि विद्या सब दीजै॥
भक्ति दान दीजै मातेश्वरी।
शरणागत को तारो अम्बे॥
विद्या धन सब ज्ञान जहाना।
तुम बिन सुन्दरि कौन ठिकाना॥
कवि कोविद रस आगम वाणी।
गावत चतुर्वेद रस बाणी॥
लिपि पढ़ाई सृजन विचारा।
सरस्वती कृपा से संसारा॥
कला कौशल सब तुमसे आवै।
बिन तव कृपा कोउ नहिं पावै॥
गणित ज्योतिष आयुर्वेदा।
वास्तु शिल्प सब तुमसे भेदा॥
कविता छन्द अलंकार रसा।
वाग्देवी कृपा सदा बरसा॥
जिन पर कृपा तुम्हारी हो जावै।
सो विद्या गुण सर्व सुख पावै॥
ज्ञान दीप प्रकाशित कीजै।
अज्ञान तिमिर सब मेट दीजै॥
सुन्दर शुभ विद्या फल दाता।
बोलो जय जय वीणा हाथा॥
माँ शारदे कृपा बरसाओ।
भव सागर से पार लगाओ॥
सदा सरस्वती मैया के गुण गाऊँ।
भवसागर से पार हो जाऊँ॥

॥ दोहा ॥
सरस्वती चालीस यह, पढ़त सदा चित लाय।
विद्या बुद्धि ज्ञान सब, मंगल सिद्धि सुहाय॥
transliteration:
|| Doha ||
Jai Saraswati Gunsadan, Vimala Veena Hath.
Shwet Kamal Par Virajati, More Hon Sahnath.
Jai Saraswati Bhagvati, Sakal Vidya Datar.
Chalisa Jo Padhat Nit, Mitahin Kasht Apar.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Jai Sur Muni Brind Bandini.
Manohar Matidayini Jai Jai.
Shwet Basan Paridhan Viraje.
Shwet Kamal Asan Par Saje.
Veena Pustak Shobhit Hatha.
Japmalika Dharat Nit Satha.
Mayur Vahan Shubh Lalama.
Pujat Brahma Vishnu Shiv Kama.
Rag Ragini Tan Suhavan.
Gyan Vigyan Sabai Gun Pavan.
Sangeet Shastra Vinodini Maiya.
Mudh Buddhi Prerat Sada Daiya.
Ved Shastra Bodhini Maiya.
Moh Nisha Ka Gyan Saveya.
Jo Saraswati Ko Dhyavat.
Nitya Prem Se Gun Gavat.
Medha Buddhi Gyan Badai.
Vidya Praveen Hohin Sukhdai.
Dasan Ko Vipada Har Leejai.
Gyan Buddhi Vidya Sab Deejai.
Bhakti Dan Deejai Mateshwari.
Sharnagat Ko Taro Ambe.
Vidya Dhan Sab Gyan Jahana.
Tum Bin Sundari Kaun Thikana.
Kavi Kovid Ras Agam Vani.
Gavat Chaturved Ras Bani.
Lipi Padhai Srijan Vichara.
Saraswati Kripa Se Sansara.
Kala Kaushal Sab Tumse Aavai.
Bin Tav Kripa Kou Nahin Pavai.
Ganit Jyotish Ayurveda.
Vastu Shilp Sab Tumse Bheda.
Kavita Chhand Alankar Rasa.
Vagdevi Kripa Sada Barsa.
Jin Par Kripa Tumhari Ho Javai.
So Vidya Gun Sarv Sukh Pavai.
Gyan Deep Prakashit Keejai.
Agyan Timir Sab Met Deejai.
Sundar Shubh Vidya Phal Data.
Bolo Jai Jai Veena Hatha.
Maa Sharade Kripa Barsao.
Bhav Sagar Se Par Lagao.
Sada Saraswati Maiya Ke Gun Gaun.
Bhav Sagar Se Par Ho Jaun.

|| Doha ||
Saraswati Chalis Yah, Padhat Sada Chit Lay.
Vidya Buddhi Gyan Sab, Mangal Siddhi Suhay.
hindiMeaning:
सरस्वती माँ की जय हो, जो गुणों की सदन (निवास) हैं, विमल (निर्मल) वीणा हाथ में धारण किए हैं, श्वेत कमल पर विराजमान हैं - वे मेरी सहायक हों। समस्त विद्या की दाता भगवती सरस्वती की जय हो, जिनका चालीसा नित्य पढ़ने से अपार कष्ट मिट जाते हैं।

सुर, मुनि और बृन्द द्वारा वन्दित, मनोहर, मतिदायिनी माँ की जय हो। श्वेत वस्त्र धारण किए शोभायमान हैं, श्वेत कमल के आसन पर सुसज्जित हैं। हाथों में वीणा और पुस्तक शोभा पा रहे हैं, सदैव जपमाला साथ धारण करती हैं। मयूर (मोर) वाहन सुशोभित है, ब्रह्मा, विष्णु, शिव और कामदेव पूजन करते हैं।
benefits: Invokes the blessing of academic excellence and intellectual growth
Invokes mastery over arts, music, and literature
Traditionally recited for removal of ignorance and mental dullness
Blesses with eloquence and clarity of speech
Essential for students and scholars seeking success
Traditionally associated with wisdom and knowledge in all sciences
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Saraswati, Saraswati Chalisa (सरस्वती चालीसा) stands as a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn. This revered text originates from Traditional. Devotees recite this prayer to grant mastery over arts, music, and literature and bestow academic excellence and intellectual growth. It is also said to bless with eloquence and clarity of speech. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Saraswati Puja. The text contains 42 verses and takes around 12 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Saraswati Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Saraswati Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration. Forty verses to Goddess Saraswati for knowledge, wisdom and academic success.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Saraswati Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The Saraswati Chalisa is a forty-verse devotional hymn (chalisa = forty in Hindi) dedicated to Goddess Saraswati, the deity of knowledge, wisdom, music, speech, and the arts. The hymn celebrates Saraswati as the consort of Brahma, the mother of the Vedas, the goddess seated on a white lotus holding a veena, a manuscript, and a mala. Each verse meditates on a specific aspect of her grace, calling her Sharada, Vagishvari, Bharati, Hamsa-vahini, and Shvetambara. The chalisa is recited daily by students, scholars, musicians, and artists across India. (Source: Drik Panchang; Astroyogi; Divine Aarti.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Saraswati Chalisa most powerfully recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Vasant Panchami (Magha Shukla Panchami, falling in late January or early February) is celebrated as the appearance day of Goddess Saraswati and the foremost day for her worship. Devotees wear yellow, decorate the puja altar with yellow flowers and yellow sweets, place books, musical instruments, and tools of learning before her image, and recite the Saraswati Chalisa as the day's principal hymn. The festival also marks the traditional Akshara Abhyasam, when small children are introduced to their first written letter under the goddess's blessing. (Source: Bhakti Bharat; Vividh Gyan; Drik Panchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What does Saraswati's iconography signify?
seoFaqs.answer: Saraswati is depicted seated on a white lotus, dressed in white or yellow, with four hands holding a veena (the lute, signifying mastery of arts and the harmony of knowledge), a pustaka (palm-leaf manuscript, signifying the Vedas and all written knowledge), an akshamala (rosary, signifying meditation and spiritual practice), and abhaya mudra (gesture of fearlessness). Her vahana is the hamsa (swan), said to possess the discrimination to separate milk from water, symbolising the discriminating intelligence (viveka) that the goddess bestows on her devotees. The Saraswati Chalisa references each of these symbols. (Source: Astroyogi; Divine Aarti; Devnaman.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does daily Saraswati Chalisa recitation bring?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional sources state that disciplined recitation grants improved memory, clarity of thought, success in education and examinations, eloquence in speech, mastery of music and arts, and the removal of intellectual stagnation. Students preparing for major exams often recite the chalisa each morning for 41 consecutive days. Musicians and dancers begin practice sessions with the chalisa. Writers and researchers invoke Saraswati before commencing creative work. The hymn is also chanted during the period between Vasant Panchami and Holi as part of a structured Saraswati sadhana. (Source: Vividh Gyan; Astroyogi; Devnaman.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Saraswati connected to the river of the same name?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vedas honour Saraswati as a river goddess, the most sacred of the seven rivers of Vedic India (Rig Veda 6.61, 7.95-96). Hymns describe her flowing from the Himalayas to the sea, sustaining civilisation along her banks. Over time, as the geological river is believed to have dried or gone subterranean, Saraswati was venerated as the inner river of consciousness flowing through the brain and spinal cord, and as the deity of all knowledge that 'flows' from teacher to student. The Saraswati Chalisa carries echoes of this riverine imagery, calling her the unceasing current of vidya. (Source: Rig Veda 6.61; Hindu-Cosmos; Drik Panchang Saraswati Mantra List.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the prescribed method for chanting the Saraswati Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Devotees bathe before sunrise, wear clean white or yellow clothing, place a Saraswati image or yantra on a clean altar, and offer white or yellow flowers, white sandalwood paste, yellow rice grains (akshata), milk, and white sweets. Books, pens, and instruments may be placed at the goddess's feet for blessing. The chalisa is recited 1, 3, 7, or 11 times, followed by the Saraswati aarti 'Om Jai Saraswati Mata.' Tuesdays and Fridays are auspicious weekly days, and the brahma-muhurta is the most powerful hour. (Source: Drik Panchang; Bhakti Bharat; Ramodey.)
